verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is an intracellular ER-resident calcium channel with a 6-transmembrane domain architecture facing the ER lumen, not the plasma membrane outer leaflet. ~85% of the protein is cytoplasmic. No evidence of plasma membrane residence, ectodomain shedding, secreted isoforms, surface trafficking, stable TM-partner tethering, or cell-state-induced surface display. The protein's documented localization is exclusively to ER membranes, including ER beneath the apical surface in polarized cells—still an intracellular compartment. No surface-directed therapeutic programs, no documented PM pool, no contextual surface biology documented.
